mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 7.0 percent, labor market slack triggers recession fears that override monetary easing, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above 7.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with accelerating consumer price inflation signals demand-pull pressure that forces central banks to tighten policy expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict return direction with a Rank IC above 0.04 over any 24-month rolling window, or returns remain positive when both series rise simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery periods, month-over-month increases in the industrial production index confirm capacity utilization improvements that drive immediate positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show negative or zero correlation with INDPRO growth specifically during post-shock recovery windows, with a t-statistic below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in housing starts indicate a collapsing construction pipeline that reduces downstream economic activity, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in over 60 percent of cases following two consecutive monthly housing starts declines.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening that boosts consumer spending expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields a Rank IC below 0.03 over rolling 12-month windows, or 5-day returns are negative when both conditions align.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation surpasses 2.5 percent annually, market participants price in imminent monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50 percent of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 2.5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product combined with rising industrial output confirms broad-based economic expansion that supports corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no predictive relationship with GDPC1 and INDPRO growth rates, with a cumulative Rank IC below 0.02 over a 3-year sample.
